items.title: Life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug to be pulled from shelves

children.content: Patients have been left frustrated and scared after learning that a life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug is to be pulled from shelves in Australia and some other countries.

gallery.altText: A box of Zoladex 3.6mg.

A woman smiles while lying in a hospital bed.

items.title: PM says Australia has 'ideological disagreement' with US after it reveals anti-slavery tariff

children.content: The planned new tariff is to be slapped on dozens of countries that have allegedly failed to take action to prevent slavery and forced labour.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Australia's economic slowdown is just beginning, experts warn

children.content: The economy grew just 0.3 per cent in the first quarter, gross domestic product per capita went backwards, and interest rate rises and cost-of-living pressures are weighing on households.

image.altText: Workers cross a Sydney street

items.title: Greens 'concerned' about Chalmers's tax change powers

children.content: The Greens have raised concerns about the ministerial discretion contained in the federal government's budget bill before parliament. Follow live.

image.altText: Greens Senator Nick McKim

items.title: US House votes to rein in Trump on Iran war

children.content: The US House of Representatives delivers President Donald Trump a rare rebuke, voting to halt military action in his war with Iran.

image.altText: Donald Trump gestures with both hands as he speaks from the Resolute Desk.

items.title: In a town where a baby died at a homeless campsite, charities say the data does not add up

children.content: As governments work to meet the growing need for social housing, community groups are stepping up to meet the rapidly increasing demand for their services to help fill the gap.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon renew ceasefire but Hezbollah not present at talks

children.content: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire, according to a joint statement with the United States.

image.altText: Smoke billows over buildings in southern Lebanon after a missile strike

items.title: British PM urges calm after protests over handcuffed Henry Nowak's death

children.content: Video of officers ignoring the pleas of a dying, innocent man has sparked a political storm about how police treat ‌different ethnicities.

image.altText: UK Police officers in yellow hi-viz clothing, helmets and behind riot shields clouded in red flare smoke.

items.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

articles.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt and long hair looks at camera.

articles.title: What's got the pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new AI technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

image.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

items.title: WHO says Ebola outbreak may have started as early as January

children.content: Militant attacks have hampered efforts to combat the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

image.altText: A person wearing full-body and face PPE including a face mask standing in a medical tent.

items.title: ASX to fall, SpaceX eyes mega IPO

children.content: The ASX is set to fall after Wall Street slipped overnight as hopes for a swift end to the war in Iran faded again. Follow the latest on our live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: Ban on travelling under bridge being reviewed amid refuelling debacle

children.content: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

gallery.altText: A large orange ship approaches a bridge span

thumbnail.altText: A large orange ship approaches a bridge span

items.title: Sportsbet owner expands from gambling into less-regulated 'rewards club'

children.content: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

image.altText: Two AFL players on a field, a sportsbet logo in the background.

items.title: World number one gutted after 'dirty' loss in French Open quarterfinal

children.content: Aryna Sabalenka feels the pain of a shock loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open, saying she just feels like smashing things.

image.altText: Aryna Sabalenka pulls a face as she carries her bags off court at the French Open.

children.content: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

items.title: 'A huge gamble': How design shows choose their projects

children.content: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

image.altText: A camera films an old building with scaffolding around it.

items.title: Quiz: Can you recognise these 10 classic Australian album covers?

children.content: Take a closer look at some of the biggest albums in Australian music and tell us what you see.

items.title: Why some home owners are at risk of slipping into negative equity

children.content: A 46-year-old former "forever renter" believes her house is now worth less than what she paid for it and she is at risk of slipping into negative equity, which would mean her Marrickville home could be worth less than what she owes the bank.

gallery.altText: A woman holding up a cup of coffee

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding up a cup of coffee

items.title: Elderly NSW couple stabbed, alleged intruder shot during home invasion

children.content: A couple in their 70s have been critically injured and a man has been shot during a suspected home invasion in northern NSW this morning.

image.altText: Images of blue and white checkered police tape stretched across a suburban street.

items.title: Ben went missing from his island home. His body has never been found

children.content: It's a tropical island, a few kilometres wide and known for being riddled with death adders. There's only one person who has ever gone missing from the island and never been found. An inquest is now trying to figure out what happened.

image.altText: A graphic of a man and a tropical background.

items.title: Water security concerns as magnetite miner seeks extension

children.content: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about ensuring there's enough groundwater to go around.

image.altText: A tank protests a mining water licence application

items.title: Cannabis driving limit change to give 'freedom' to medicinal users in NSW

children.content: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

image.altText: Police officers testing drivers for cannabis near Nimbin

items.title: A year since the varroa mite entered Qld, honey bee colonies have collapsed

children.content: Beekeepers in Queensland say they are 'barely hanging on' a year after the varroa mite was detected in the state for the first time.

items.title: 'A little perturbed': Trump confirms expletive-laden call with Netanyahu

children.content: In an interview with a US podcast, the US president says he was frustrated that Benjamin Netanyahu was "constantly fighting with Lebanon".

image.altText: Donald Trump gestures next to Benjamin Netanyahu who holds his thumbs up.

items.title: UN envoy intervenes in Woodside gas plant challenge

children.content: An independent expert has urged Australia to "make sure that things are done properly", while Woodside pushed back against her involvement.

image.altText: A Hispanic woman with black hair and dressed in a white jacket and colourful scarf sits before a microphone on a panel.

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Foster questions 'chilling' silence from World Cup players on human rights

children.content: Human rights advocate and former Socceroos captain Craig Foster says he is concerned by the lack of players speaking out about the United States' human rights record.

image.altText: Former Socceroos player Craig Foster gestures as he speaks.

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security. But it's not only in summer heatwaves are occurring and causing crops to fail.

items.title: Tour operators brace for Kakadu site closures 'like COVID all over again'

children.content: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

image.altText: An aerial shot of Jim Jim Falls

items.title: Child sex offender given 'disability pride' award after admin error

children.content: Repeat child sex offender Cameron Bloomfield was crowned as Victoria's "disability pride champion" in 2024, after a government department admin error.

image.altText: Cameron Bloomfield stands outside in dappled light, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Call to increase no-fish, no-drill zones as marine park review looms

children.content: Australia's dive industry has backed calls to expand no-fishing and no-drilling zones across the nation's oceans ahead of a federal review of marine parks.

items.title: Police allege corporate boss refused blood alcohol tests after hitting five cars

children.content: Private equity baron Joel Thickins is alleged to have crashed his BMW into five cars in Sydney's east, before refusing two alcohol-detecting breath tests.

items.title: Injury-plagued star forced into another injury withdrawal at Roland Garros

children.content: Matteo Berrettini is once again forced out of a match by injury, retiring in the second set of his French Open quarterfinal with compatriot Matteo Arnaldi.

image.altText: Matteo Berrettini acknowledges the crowd as he leaves the French Open.

items.title: The NBA's new face is here and he is unlike anything we've ever seen

children.content: After years of searching, the NBA finally has its new face in Victor Wembanyama, and he is about to grace the league's biggest stage.
